commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `navigateViaGoToFolder` so the Cmd+Shift+G and Cmd+A `InputDriver.hotkey` calls throw instead of silently swallowing errors.

Reproducibility: no. live high-confidence reproduction was run. Source inspection shows the failure path: current main discards thrown hotkey errors in `navigateViaGoToFolder` before typing the directory and pressing Return.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof or maintainer override before merge; no automated repair is indicated because the patch itself has no line-level defect.

target.title: fix(dialog): propagate hotkey failures in Go-to-Folder navigation
target.body:
## Summary

`navigateViaGoToFolder()` in `DialogService+FileDialogNavigation.swift` uses `try?` on two critical input-synthesis calls: Cmd+Shift+G (open Go-to-Folder sheet) and Cmd+A (select existing text). If either hotkey fails, the error is silently discarded and the method continues, typing the directory path into whatever field currently has focus and pressing Return.

## Problem

When `InputDriver.hotkey` fails for Cmd+Shift+G (line 212), the Go-to-Folder sheet never opens. The method then:

1. Re-asserts dialog focus (line 216)
2. Sends Cmd+A to select text (line 218, also `try?`)
3. Types the directory path (line 220)
4. Presses Return (line 221)

Without the Go-to-Folder sheet, these keystrokes act on the main dialog, potentially navigating to a wrong location or triggering an unintended action. All three callers (lines 145, 175, 183) use `try await`, expecting errors to propagate.

## Fix

Replace `try?` with `try` on both hotkey calls. The method already declares `async throws`, and all three callers already handle thrown errors via `try await`. Adjacent calls in the same method (`typeTextValue` at line 220, `tapKey` at line 221) already use `try`.

## Origin

Introduced in commit `abf29e33` (Peter Steinberger, 2026-05-07, "refactor(dialog): split file dialog helpers"). The `try?` was likely carried over from a different context during the refactoring. Present for 54 days.

## Related

- #193 (merged): same `try?` swallowing bug class in `WatchCaptureSession+Loop.swift`
- #203 (open): same pattern in `DaemonLaunchPolicy.swift` daemon poll loops
- #204 (open): same pattern in `Speech.swift` recorder observer

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `try?` on Cmd+Shift+G and Cmd+A hotkeys in `navigateViaGoToFolder()` silently discards input-synthesis failures. If the Go-to-Folder sheet fails to open, the method types the path into the wrong field and submits the dialog.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Swift 6.3.3 (swiftlang-6.3.3.1.3), built from patched source on `fix/dialog-hotkey-cancellation` branch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```bash
  cd /Users/sebastientardif/Peekaboo
  git checkout fix/dialog-hotkey-cancellation

  # Step 1. Build to verify compilation
  swift build --package-path Core/PeekabooAutomationKit

  # Step 2. Verify fix in source
  grep -n 'InputDriver.hotkey' \
    Core/PeekabooAutomationKit/Sources/PeekabooAutomationKit/Services/UI/DialogService+FileDialogNavigation.swift

  # Step 3. Verify method signature and callers
  grep -n 'navigateViaGoToFolder' \
    Core/PeekabooAutomationKit/Sources/PeekabooAutomationKit/Services/UI/DialogService+FileDialogNavigation.swift
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build:

  ```console
  $ swift build --package-path Core/PeekabooAutomationKit
  Building for debugging...
  Build complete! (0.30s)
  ```

  Fix verified in source (both hotkey calls now use `try` instead of `try?`):

  ```console
  $ grep -n 'InputDriver.hotkey' Core/PeekabooAutomationKit/Sources/PeekabooAutomationKit/Services/UI/DialogService+FileDialogNavigation.swift
  158:            try? InputDriver.hotkey(keys: ["cmd", "a"], holdDuration: 0.05)
  212:        try InputDriver.hotkey(keys: ["cmd", "shift", "g"], holdDuration: 0.05)
  218:        try InputDriver.hotkey(keys: ["cmd", "a"], holdDuration: 0.05)
  ```

  Line 158 is a separate best-effort call in the path-text-field fallback (not part of this fix). Lines 212 and 218 now propagate errors.

  All three callers use `try await`, confirming errors propagate correctly:

  ```console
  $ grep -n 'navigateViaGoToFolder' Core/PeekabooAutomationKit/Sources/PeekabooAutomationKit/Services/UI/DialogService+FileDialogNavigation.swift
  145:            try await self.navigateViaGoToFolder(directoryPath: requestedDirectory, dialog: dialog, appName: appName)
  175:                try await self.navigateViaGoToFolder(
  183:            try await self.navigateViaGoToFolder(
  206:    private func navigateViaGoToFolder(directoryPath: String, dialog: Element, appName: String?) async throws {
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Build succeeds with `try` on both hotkey calls. The method signature (`async throws`) and all three callers (`try await`) already support error propagation. The fix aligns lines 212 and 218 with adjacent calls (`typeTextValue` at 220, `tapKey` at 221) that already use `try`.
- **What was not tested:** Live UI automation with an active file dialog (requires macOS accessibility permissions, a running application, and a triggered hotkey failure). This is a 2-character mechanical fix (removing `?` from `try?`) that the compiler verifies at build time.
target.author: sebtardif
